title: The Bonnie Wee Thing.
content:
Tune - "Bonnie wee thing."


I.

        Bonnie wee thing, cannie wee thing,
            Lovely wee thing, wert thou mine,
        I wad wear thee in my bosom,
            Lest my jewel I should tine.
        Wishfully I look and languish
            In that bonnie face o' thine;
        And my heart it stounds wi' anguish,
            Lest my wee thing be na mine.

II.

        Wit, and grace, and love, and beauty
            In ae constellation shine;
        To adore thee is my duty,
            Goddess o' this soul o' mine!
        Bonnie wee thing, cannie wee thing.
            Lovely wee thing, wert thou mine,
        I wad wear thee in my bosom,
            Lest my jewel I should tine!
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Bonnie Wee Thing."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
